title: rosa micrantha var. lemanii
intro: Rosa micrantha var. lemanii, also known as the dwarf alpine rose, is a shrub-like plant species native to the mountains of Europe, characterized by its small, pink flowers and thorny stems. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Rosa micrantha var. lemanii have been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
